For this reason,most apothecaries keep sufficient quantities of antidotes close at hand. Antidotes work in one-third (round down) the minim um onset time of the poison they coimteract. Theseantidotes cost (in terms of time and gold) just as much as their respective poison to researchand make. However, most apothecaries sell them for half the cost of their corresponding poison. [PDF_PAGE 79 | score=2] Plnyrr’s Hsmilzroolrfor hill details about how the healer canuse theseproficiencies to aid patients in healing in a natural, nonmagical manner. As the healer progressesin levels, he must fill any new nonweapon proficiency slots with either the Healing or the Herbalism proficiencies. in this way, the healer actually progressesin his chosenprofession. Additionally, the healer canchangeany earned weapon proficiency into a nonweapon proficiency after 1stlevel. Theseslots are exchangedon a one-for-one basis:l weapon proficiency slot is worth 1 nonweapon profi- ciency slot. Thesetransformed proficiencies do not have to be spent on Herbalism or Heal- ing, but many healerschooseto concentrate all of
[PDF_PAGE 13 | score=1] it proficiency slot every three levels after that (at the 3rd level, 6th level, and so on). The NPC canchoosefrom the wizard and general proficiencies without any additional slot costs. 7¢ew The apothecary’s main calling is the concocting of new miztu res.He createsthese things through useof the new Concocting proficiency. An apothecary must useone of his lst level nonweapon proficiency slots to select the Concocting proficiency. ‘Someapothecaries chooseto spend nearly all of their slots on this proficiency. Note that this proficiency is available to members of the apothecary NFC classonlv. No other classrnav choosethis nonweapon proficiency. n tif1:1:-rcorﬁ:.:r-;- This proﬁciency enablesthe
[PDF_PAGE 14 | score=1] lf the apothecary rolls a 20 when identifying a particular material, his result will be significantly inaccurate.Sometimes, this meansthat he identifies a ix"-tilingpotion asa poison or vice versa,but it could also mean that he identifies a potionof ESPasa potiim of rea’dragoncontrol.In thesecases,the DM should do his absolute best to convince the players that the apothecary correctly identified the material. To usethe Concocting nonweapon proficiency, the apothecary must have a well- appointed lab at his disposal. Additionally, if the apothecary wishes to concoctsometh.in.g, he must have all of the ingredients on hand. An apothecary must make a proficiency checkonly when attempting to ident
[PDF_PAGE 25 | score=1] Appraisers are people who know how to identify items and establish a reasonable approximation of their fair market value. Often they enter business as merchants, pmchasing items brought to them and then selling the items for maximum profit. Appraisers usually pay their clients about half of what they believe they can get for the item in question, making their living on the substantial profit margins. Seeclyappraisers, those not afraid to deal with the poorer elements of their neighborhoods, sometimes work as pawn brokers. They hand out loans for about half the value of an item used as collateral. If the client defaults on the loan, as often happens, the pawn broker gets to keep, and then sel
[PDF_PAGE 84 | score=1] —\_ Gnarvis the Healer Male Human Healer Str: ll Dex: 9 Con: 15 Int: 12 Wis: lf-l Cha: ll Armor Class: .7(relics +3) Level: ~l Tl-IACD: 19 Movement: 12 Hit Points: 20 Alignment: Lawful Good No. of Attacks: l Special Attacks: None Special Defenses: N one Weapon Proficiencies: Dagger, staff. Nonweapon Proficiencies: Healing (I7), Herbalism (10). Equipment: Rah.-rs+3,healing herbs,staff, knife (surgical). Gnarvis is a yoimg journeyman healer who graduated from his apprenticeship just over a year ago. After leaving his master, Gnarvis decided to return to his family's ancestral home in the bayou portion of the necromancers sw amp. Most of the area had long sincebeen twisted into something unholy
